what_it_does: Creates the 'ledger' Postgres schema for the-one-system: a run-scoped structural code/design ledger with four tables (run, entry, symbol, edge), idempotent additive ALTERs, a set of indexes (including trigram and per-run), and accumulation views (latest_run, entry_latest, symbol_latest, edge_latest, coverage) that surface only the most recent complete run per (project, purpose).
observations: Enables extensions vector, pg_trgm and ltree; creates schema ledger if not exists.
ledger.run is the run header scoped by project/channel/purpose/layer with provenance (model, prompt_version, tool_git_sha, tool_version, schema_version), status, stats jsonb and timestamps.

ledger.entry is the per-file/node row with rich interpretive columns (what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty) plus structural fields (imports, declares, address_schemes_used, stores_touched, env_vars, markers, signals), unique(run_id, path).
ledger.symbol holds per-symbol code detail (signature, params, returns, decorators, bases, line range, is_exported/is_async, does, description), unique(run_id, code_id).
ledger.edge holds typed relations (from_ref, kind, to_raw, to_resolved, line) with provenance.
Idempotent additive ALTER TABLE ... add column if not exists for extractor/tool provenance columns on already-existing schemas.

Header states it supersedes the loose build-prep/the-one-system/ledger_schema.sql (retired to a pointer), is reversible via DROP SCHEMA ledger CASCADE, and reuses the code:// coordinate; runs ACCUMULATE for drift comparison.
Views use distinct on (project, purpose) over status='complete' to define the 'latest run' projection; coverage groups file entries by coverage_state.
standouts: The non-flat, run-scoped accumulation design: every extraction is a RUN, runs accumulate and are comparable over time (drift), with latest-* views giving a usable current view over the accumulation.
The entry table's interpretive columns mirror exactly the interpretation JSON schema this very wave produces (what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, intention_signals, etc.).
conventions: Fully idempotent: create extension/schema/table if not exists, add column if not exists, create index if not exists, create or replace view.
Per-row provenance and per-extractor version columns on run/entry/symbol.
jsonb for open/extensible structured fields (signals, markers, extra, suggested_fields, proposed_edge_kinds).
notes: This is the canonical durable home for the-one-system's interpretation/extraction ledger — the run/entry/symbol/edge schema plus latest-run views that the interpretation pipeline writes into and queries.
questions: What enumerates the allowed values for node_type, coverage_state, kind and edge.kind (no CHECK constraints or lookup tables are defined here)?
The ltree extension is enabled but no ltree column appears in these tables — where is it used?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: header describes a non-flat run-scoped structural ledger with provenance and accumulation, and the four tables plus latest-* views implement precisely that.
apparent_themes: Run-scoped accumulation enabling drift comparison over time
Provenance on every row (model, prompt, tool sha, extractor version)
intention_signals: Trying to give the code/design interpretation pipeline a durable, queryable, version-aware home where extractions are runs that accumulate, so the same corpus can be re-mapped and compared rather than overwritten.
novelty: Treating every extraction as a first-class scoped RUN with accumulation + drift semantics, and an entry table whose interpretive columns directly match the per-file interpretation contract.
contribution: Contributes the entire ledger schema (run/entry/symbol/edge tables, provenance columns, trigram/per-run indexes, and latest-run accumulation views) that persists and serves the-one-system's interpretation output.
summary_for_embedding: Supabase migration creating the run-scoped 'ledger' schema (run, entry, symbol, edge tables plus latest-run and coverage views) to persist accumulating, provenance-tagged code/design interpretation extractions.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a durable, queryable, version-aware ledger where code interpretation extractions are scoped runs that accumulate for drift comparison rather than being overwritten.
